description:
# New ir_reducer pass; walks from roots and inlines static single-clause calls

## Goal

A new module `crate::ir_reducer` exporting `reduce_module(&mut Module)`.
Walks the call graph from program roots (`main` + every fn passed to
`spawn` + every exported entry). For each callsite:

- If callee is a single non-recursive clause AND every input is
  statically known (literal Descr / closure_lit / etc.):
  inline the callee's body with bindings substituted; constant-fold
  the result using fz-RED.1's primitive.
- Otherwise: leave the call in place.

**No recursion across calls yet** — that lands in RED.4. This
ticket scaffolds the pass and proves the single-clause case.

## Pipeline integration

```
AST → ir_lower → ir_inline → ir_fold → ir_dce → ir_fuse
   → ir_reducer (NEW)
   → typer → codegen
```

Reducer runs AFTER existing inline/fold/dce/fuse so it sees a
clean call graph. The typer then sees the reduced Module.

## Acceptance

- `polymorphic` fixture: 0 `id` bodies emitted (was 3+ specs).
- `higher_order` fixture: `apply2(double, 21)` reduces to `42`
  (when `double` is a single-clause leaf and `apply2` is too).
- `closure_typed_captures` and `curried_add` partially reduce
  (full reduction lands in RED.5).
- All other fixtures unchanged.
- `cargo test` green; per-fixture golden goldens re-blessed only
  for fixtures explicitly listed above (otherwise must be byte-
  identical).
- Interp + JIT + AOT agree on output (three-path parity).

## Removals

None yet. `ir_inline` continues to run; RED.8c removes it once
RED.4 and RED.5 have absorbed its work.
title: fz-RED.3: reducer pass scaffold (single-clause inline)
